1	"""
2	Lightning module for multi-type blind EQ parameter estimation.
3	Uses the streaming TCN model with multi-type DSP and permutation-invariant loss.
4	"""
5	import sys
6	import torch
7	import pytorch_lightning as pl
8	from pathlib import Path
9	
10	sys.path.append(str(Path(__file__).parent.parent))
11	
12	from model_tcn import StreamingTCNModel
13	from dsp_frontend import STFTFrontend
14	from loss_multitype import MultiTypeEQLoss
15	
16	
17	class EQEstimatorLightning(pl.LightningModule):
18	    """
19	    Lightning Module for training the IDSP multi-type EQ parameter estimator.
20	    """
21	
22	    def __init__(self, config):
23	        super().__init__()
24	        self.save_hyperparameters()
25	        self.config = config
26	
27	        data_cfg = config["data"]
28	        model_cfg = config["model"]
29	        loss_cfg = config["loss"]
30	
31	        # Model
32	        self.model = StreamingTCNModel(
33	            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
34	            embedding_dim=model_cfg["encoder"].get("embedding_dim", 128),
35	            num_bands=data_cfg.get("num_bands", 5),
36	            channels=model_cfg["encoder"].get("channels", 128),
37	            num_blocks=model_cfg["encoder"].get("num_blocks", 4),
38	            num_stacks=model_cfg["encoder"].get("num_stacks", 2),
39	            sample_rate=data_cfg.get("sample_rate", 44100),
40	            n_fft=data_cfg.get("n_fft", 2048),
41	        )
42	
43	        # STFT frontend for audio→mel conversion
44	        self.frontend = STFTFrontend(
45	            n_fft=data_cfg.get("n_fft", 2048),
46	            hop_length=data_cfg.get("hop_length", 256),
47	            win_length=data_cfg.get("n_fft", 2048),
48	            mel_bins=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
49	            sample_rate=data_cfg.get("sample_rate", 44100),
50	        )
51	
52	        # Loss
53	        self.criterion = MultiTypeEQLoss(
54	            n_fft=data_cfg.get("n_fft", 2048),
55	            sample_rate=data_cfg.get("sample_rate", 44100),
56	            lambda_param=loss_cfg.get("lambda_param", 1.0),
57	            lambda_type=loss_cfg.get("lambda_type", 0.5),
58	            lambda_spectral=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spectral", 1.0),
59	            lambda_hmag=loss_cfg.get("lambda_hmag", 0.3),
60	            lambda_activity=loss_cfg.get("lambda_activity", 0.1),
61	            lambda_spread=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spread", 0.05),
62	        )
63	
64	    def forward(self, mel_frames):
65	        return self.model(mel_frames)
66	
67	    def _common_step(self, batch, batch_idx, mode="train"):
68	        wet_audio = batch["wet_audio"]
69	        dry_audio = batch["dry_audio"]
70	        target_gain = batch["gain"]
71	        target_freq = batch["freq"]
72	        target_q = batch["q"]
73	        target_ft = batch["filter_type"]
74	
75	        # Convert wet audio to mel-spectrogram
76	        mel_spec = self.frontend.mel_spectrogram(wet_audio)  # (B, 1, n_mels, T)
77	        mel_frames = mel_spec.squeeze(1)  # (B, n_mels, T)
78	
79	        # Forward pass through TCN model
80	        output = self.model(mel_frames)
81	
82	        pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
83	        pred_type_logits = output["type_logits"]
84	        pred_H_mag = output["H_mag"]
85	
86	        # Ground truth frequency response
87	        target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
88	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
89	            n_fft=self.config["data"].get("n_fft", 2048),
90	            filter_type=target_ft
91	        )
92	
93	        # Synthesize pred_audio using the predicted parameters
94	        pred_audio = self.model.dsp_cascade.process_audio(
95	            dry_audio, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q, filter_type=pred_type_logits.argmax(dim=-1)
96	        )
97	        # Ensure audio length matches for STFT
98	        target_audio = wet_audio
99	
100	        # Compute loss
101	        total_loss, components = self.criterion(
102	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
103	            pred_type_logits, pred_H_mag,
104	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
105	            target_ft, target_H_mag,
106	            pred_audio=pred_audio, target_audio=target_audio
107	        )
108	
109	        # Logging
110	        self.log(f"{mode}/total_loss", total_loss, on_step=(mode == "train"),
111	                 on_epoch=True, prog_bar=True, sync_dist=True)
112	        for k, v in components.items():
113	            self.log(f"{mode}/{k}", v, on_step=(mode == "train"),
114	                     on_epoch=True, sync_dist=True)
115	
116	        # Log parameter accuracy metrics
117	        with torch.no_grad():
118	            gain_mae = (pred_gain - target_gain).abs().mean()
119	            freq_oct = (torch.log2(pred_freq / (target_freq + 1e-8))).abs().mean()
120	            q_dec = (torch.log10(pred_q / (target_q + 1e-8))).abs().mean()
121	            type_acc = (output["filter_type"] == target_ft).float().mean()
122	
123	            self.log(f"{mode}/gain_mae_db", gain_mae, on_epoch=True)
124	            self.log(f"{mode}/freq_mae_oct", freq_oct, on_epoch=True)
125	            self.log(f"{mode}/q_mae_dec", q_dec, on_epoch=True)
126	            self.log(f"{mode}/type_acc", type_acc, on_epoch=True)
127	
128	        return total_loss
129	
130	    def training_step(self, batch, batch_idx):
131	        return self._common_step(batch, batch_idx, mode="train")
132	
133	    def validation_step(self, batch, batch_idx):
134	        return self._common_step(batch, batch_idx, mode="val")
135	
136	    def test_step(self, batch, batch_idx):
137	        return self._common_step(batch, batch_idx, mode="test")
138	
139	    def configure_optimizers(self):
140	        optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
141	            self.parameters(),
142	            lr=self.config["model"]["learning_rate"],
143	            weight_decay=self.config["model"]["weight_decay"]
144	        )
145	        scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
146	            optimizer, T_max=self.config["trainer"]["max_epochs"]
147	        )
148	        return {
149	            "optimizer": optimizer,
150	            "lr_scheduler": scheduler
151	        }
152

2	import argparse
3	import yaml
4	from pathlib import Path
5	import pytorch_lightning as pl
6	from pytorch_lightning.callbacks import ModelCheckpoint, EarlyStopping, LearningRateMonitor
7	from pytorch_lightning.loggers import WandbLogger

9	from lightning_module import EQEstimatorLightning

12	def load_config(config_path):
13	    with open(config_path, 'r') as f:
14	        return yaml.safe_load(f)
15	
16	def main():
17	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
18	    parser.add_argument("--config", type=str, default="../conf/config.yaml")
19	    parser.add_argument("--data_dir", type=str, required=True)
20	    args = parser.parse_args()
21	    
22	    config = load_config(args.config)
23	    
24	    # 1. Set seed
25	    pl.seed_everything(config['seed'])
26	    
27	    # 2. Setup Data
28	    train_loader, val_loader, test_loader = create_dataloaders(
29	        data_dir=args.data_dir,
30	        batch_size=config['data']['batch_size'],
31	        num_workers=config['data']['num_workers'],
32	        split=(config['data']['train_split'], config['data']['val_split'], config['data']['test_split'])
33	    )
34	    
35	    # 3. Init Model
36	    model = EQEstimatorLightning(config)
37	    
38	    # 4. Callbacks & Logger
39	    wandb_logger = WandbLogger(project=config['experiment_name'], log_model="all")
40	    
41	    checkpoint_callback = ModelCheckpoint(
42	        monitor="val/total_loss",
43	        dirpath="checkpoints",
44	        filename="eq-model-{epoch:02d}-{val_loss:.2f}",
45	        save_top_k=3,
46	        mode="min",
47	    )
48	    
49	    early_stop_callback = EarlyStopping(
50	        monitor="val/total_loss",
51	        min_delta=0.001,
52	        patience=10,
53	        verbose=True,
54	        mode="min"
55	    )
56	    
57	    # 5. Trainer
58	    trainer = pl.Trainer(
59	        max_epochs=config['trainer']['max_epochs'],
60	        accelerator=config['trainer']['accelerator'],
61	        devices=config['trainer']['devices'],
62	        precision=config['trainer']['precision'],
63	        logger=wandb_logger,
64	        callbacks=[checkpoint_callback, early_stop_callback, LearningRateMonitor(logging_interval='step')],
65	        fast_dev_run=config['trainer']['fast_dev_run'],
66	        log_every_n_steps=config['trainer']['log_every_n_steps'],
67	    )
68	    
69	    # 6. Fit
70	    trainer.fit(model, train_loader, val_loader)
71	    
72	    # 7. Test
73	    trainer.test(model, test_loader)
74	
75	if __name__ == "__main__":
76	    main()

1	"""
2	Curriculum learning callback for multi-type EQ estimation.
3	
4	Manages staged training with increasing difficulty:
5	1. Peaking-only warmup
6	2. Multi-type with high type loss weight
7	3. Full difficulty with spectral consistency
8	4. Real-world fine-tuning
9	"""
10	import torch
11	import pytorch_lightning as pl
12	
13	
14	class CurriculumCallback(pl.Callback):
15	    """
16	    Lightning callback that manages curriculum learning stages.
17	    Adjusts loss weights, parameter ranges, and Gumbel-Softmax temperature.
18	    """
19	
20	    def __init__(self, config):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.stages = config.get("curriculum", {}).get("stages", [])
23	        self.current_stage_idx = 0
24	
25	    def _compute_stage(self, epoch):
26	        """Determine which curriculum stage we're in based on epoch."""
27	        cumulative = 0
28	        for i, stage in enumerate(self.stages):
29	            cumulative += stage.get("epochs", 10)
30	            if epoch < cumulative:
31	                return i
32	        return len(self.stages) - 1
33	
34	    def on_train_epoch_start(self, trainer, pl_module):
35	        stage_idx = self._compute_stage(trainer.current_epoch)
36	
37	        if stage_idx != self.current_stage_idx:
38	            self.current_stage_idx = stage_idx
39	            stage = self.stages[stage_idx]
40	            print(f"\n[Curriculum] Entering stage {stage_idx}: {stage.get('name', 'unnamed')}")
41	
42	            # Update loss weights
43	            lambda_type = stage.get("lambda_type", 0.5)
44	            pl_module.criterion.lambda_type = lambda_type
45	
46	            # Update Gumbel-Softmax temperature
47	            temperature = stage.get("gumbel_temperature", 0.5)
48	            pl_module.model.param_head.gumbel_temperature.fill_(temperature)
49	
50	            # Update learning rate if specified
51	            lr_scale = stage.get("learning_rate_scale", None)
52	            if lr_scale is not None:
53	                for pg in trainer.optimizers[0].param_groups:
54	                    pg["lr"] = pl_module.config["model"]["learning_rate"] * lr_scale
55

1	"""
2	Evaluation script for the multi-type EQ estimator.
3	
4	Computes per-parameter accuracy metrics, spectral reconstruction,
5	filter type confusion, and generates visualization plots.
6	"""
7	import sys
8	import json
9	import torch
10	import numpy as np
11	from pathlib import Path
12	from tqdm import tqdm
13	
14	sys.path.append(str(Path(__file__).parent.parent))
15	
16	from model_tcn import StreamingTCNModel
17	from dsp_frontend import STFTFrontend
18	from dataset_pipeline.dataset import create_dataloaders
19	
20	
21	def evaluate_model(model, dataloader, frontend, device="cuda", n_fft=2048):
22	    """
23	    Evaluate model on a dataset.
24	
25	    Returns dict of metrics and per-sample predictions.
26	    """
27	    model.eval()
28	    model.to(device)
29	
30	    all_gain_pred = []
31	    all_gain_gt = []
32	    all_freq_pred = []
33	    all_freq_gt = []
34	    all_q_pred = []
35	    all_q_gt = []
36	    all_type_pred = []
37	    all_type_gt = []
38	
39	    with torch.no_grad():
40	        for batch in tqdm(dataloader, desc="Evaluating"):
41	            wet_audio = batch["wet_audio"].to(device)
42	
43	            target_gain = batch["gain"]
44	            target_freq = batch["freq"]
45	            target_q = batch["q"]
46	            target_ft = batch["filter_type"]
47	
48	            # Convert to mel
49	            mel_spec = frontend.mel_spectrogram(wet_audio)
50	            mel_frames = mel_spec.squeeze(1)
51	
52	            # Forward
53	            output = model(mel_frames)
54	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
55	
56	            all_gain_pred.append(pred_gain.cpu())
57	            all_gain_gt.append(target_gain)
58	            all_freq_pred.append(pred_freq.cpu())
59	            all_freq_gt.append(target_freq)
60	            all_q_pred.append(pred_q.cpu())
61	            all_q_gt.append(target_q)
62	            all_type_pred.append(output["filter_type"].cpu())
63	            all_type_gt.append(target_ft)
64	
65	    # Concatenate
66	    gain_pred = torch.cat(all_gain_pred)
67	    gain_gt = torch.cat(all_gain_gt)
68	    freq_pred = torch.cat(all_freq_pred)
69	    freq_gt = torch.cat(all_freq_gt)
70	    q_pred = torch.cat(all_q_pred)
71	    q_gt = torch.cat(all_q_gt)
72	    type_pred = torch.cat(all_type_pred)
73	    type_gt = torch.cat(all_type_gt)
74	
75	    # Compute metrics
76	    metrics = {}
77	
78	    # Gain MAE (dB)
79	    metrics["gain_mae_db"] = (gain_pred - gain_gt).abs().mean().item()
80	    metrics["gain_median_ae_db"] = (gain_pred - gain_gt).abs().median().item()
81	
82	    # Frequency MAE (octaves)
83	    freq_oct = (torch.log2(freq_pred / (freq_gt + 1e-8))).abs()
84	    metrics["freq_mae_oct"] = freq_oct.mean().item()
85	    metrics["freq_median_ae_oct"] = freq_oct.median().item()
86	
87	    # Q MAE (decades)
88	    q_dec = (torch.log10(q_pred / (q_gt + 1e-8))).abs()
89	    metrics["q_mae_dec"] = q_dec.mean().item()
90	    metrics["q_median_ae_dec"] = q_dec.median().item()
91	
92	    # Filter type accuracy
93	    metrics["type_accuracy"] = (type_pred == type_gt).float().mean().item()
94	
95	    # Per-type accuracy
96	    FILTER_NAMES = ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
97	    for i, name in enumerate(FILTER_NAMES):
98	        mask = type_gt == i
99	        if mask.sum() > 0:
100	            metrics[f"type_acc_{name}"] = (type_pred[mask] == type_gt[mask]).float().mean().item()
101	
102	    # Confusion matrix
103	    num_types = 5
104	    confusion = torch.zeros(num_types, num_types)
105	    for p, t in zip(type_pred.flatten(), type_gt.flatten()):
106	        confusion[t.item(), p.item()] += 1
107	    metrics["confusion_matrix"] = confusion.tolist()
108	
109	    return metrics
110	
111	
112	def print_metrics(metrics):
113	    """Print evaluation metrics in a readable format."""
114	    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
115	    print("EVALUATION RESULTS")
116	    print("=" * 60)
117	
118	    print(f"\nParameter Estimation:")
119	    print(f"  Gain MAE:        {metrics['gain_mae_db']:.2f} dB (target: < 2.0)")
120	    print(f"  Gain Median AE:  {metrics['gain_median_ae_db']:.2f} dB")
121	    print(f"  Freq MAE:        {metrics['freq_mae_oct']:.3f} oct (target: < 0.5)")
122	    print(f"  Freq Median AE:  {metrics['freq_median_ae_oct']:.3f} oct")
123	    print(f"  Q MAE:           {metrics['q_mae_dec']:.3f} decades (target: < 0.3)")
124	    print(f"  Q Median AE:     {metrics['q_median_ae_dec']:.3f} decades")
125	
126	    print(f"\nFilter Type Classification:")
127	    print(f"  Overall Accuracy: {metrics['type_accuracy']:.1%} (target: > 85%)")
128	    for name in ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]:
129	        key = f"type_acc_{name}"

31	class Trainer:
32	    def __init__(self, config_path="conf/config.yaml"):
33	        self.cfg = load_config(config_path)
34	        self.device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")

36	        data_cfg = self.cfg["data"]

41	        self.num_bands = data_cfg["num_bands"]
42	        self.sample_rate = data_cfg["sample_rate"]
43	        self.n_fft = data_cfg.get("n_fft", 2048)
44	        self.log_every = trainer_cfg["log_every_n_steps"]
45	
46	        # Curriculum config
47	        self.curriculum_cfg = self.cfg.get("curriculum", {})
48	        self.stages = self.curriculum_cfg.get("stages", [])
49	
50	        # Compute total epochs from curriculum stages (or fall back to trainer.max_epochs)
51	        if self.stages:
52	            self.max_epochs = sum(s["epochs"] for s in self.stages)
53	        else:
54	            self.max_epochs = trainer_cfg["max_epochs"]
55	
56	        # Model
57	        enc_cfg = model_cfg.get("encoder", {})
58	        self.model = StreamingTCNModel(
59	            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
60	            embedding_dim=enc_cfg.get("embedding_dim", 128),
61	            num_bands=self.num_bands,
62	            channels=enc_cfg.get("channels", 128),
63	            num_blocks=enc_cfg.get("num_blocks", 4),
64	            num_stacks=enc_cfg.get("num_stacks", 2),
65	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
66	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
67	        ).to(self.device)
68	
69	        # STFT frontend
70	        self.frontend = STFTFrontend(

72	            hop_length=data_cfg.get("hop_length", 256),
73	            win_length=self.n_fft,
74	            mel_bins=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
75	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
76	        ).to(self.device)
77	
78	        # Loss (base weights — curriculum stages can override)
79	        self.base_loss_cfg = loss_cfg
80	        self.criterion = MultiTypeEQLoss(
81	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
82	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
83	            lambda_param=loss_cfg.get("lambda_param", 1.0),
84	            lambda_type=loss_cfg.get("lambda_type", 2.0),
85	            lambda_spectral=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spectral", 1.0),
86	            lambda_hmag=loss_cfg.get("lambda_hmag", 0.3),
87	            lambda_activity=loss_cfg.get("lambda_activity", 0.1),
88	            lambda_spread=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spread", 0.05),
89	        ).to(self.device)
90	
91	        self.optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
92	            self.model.parameters(),
93	            lr=model_cfg["learning_rate"],
94	            weight_decay=model_cfg["weight_decay"],
95	        )
96	
97	        self.data_cfg = data_cfg
98	        self.global_step = 0
99	        self.best_val_loss = float("inf")
100	        self.history = []
101	
102	        # Data config for stage transitions
103	        self.train_loader = None
104	        self.val_loader = None
105	        self.test_loader = None

107	    def _build_stage_dataset(self, stage_cfg):
108	        """Build a dataset for a specific curriculum stage."""
109	        data_cfg = self.data_cfg
110	
111	        # Determine which filter types to include
112	        filter_types = stage_cfg.get("filter_types", data_cfg["filter_types"])
113	        # Build type_weights: uniform over active types, 0 for inactive
114	        active_indices = [FILTER_TYPE_TO_IDX[ft] for ft in filter_types]
115	        n_active = len(active_indices)
116	        type_weights = [0.0] * len(FILTER_NAMES)
117	        for idx in active_indices:
118	            type_weights[idx] = 1.0 / n_active
119	
120	        # Get param ranges for this stage (or fall back to data config)
121	        param_ranges = stage_cfg.get("param_ranges", {})
122	        gain_lo, gain_hi = param_ranges.get("gain", data_cfg["gain_bounds"])
123	        # q is a list of [lo, hi]
124	        q_range = param_ranges.get("q", data_cfg["q_bounds"])
125	
126	        dataset = SyntheticEQDataset(
127	            num_bands=self.num_bands,
128	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
129	            duration=1.5,
130	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
131	            size=50000,
132	            gain_range=(gain_lo, gain_hi),
133	            freq_range=tuple(data_cfg["freq_bounds"]),
134	            q_range=tuple(q_range),
135	            type_weights=type_weights,
136	            precompute_mels=True,
137	            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
138	        )
139	        dataset.precompute()
140	        return dataset

142	    def _setup_stage(self, stage_cfg):
143	        """Prepare dataset and update loss weights for a curriculum stage."""
144	        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
145	        print(f"  Stage: {stage_cfg['name']}")
146	        print(f"{'='*60}")
147	
148	        # Only rebuild dataset if we don't have one yet
149	        if self.train_loader is None:
150	            dataset = self._build_stage_dataset(stage_cfg)
151	
152	            n_train = int(len(dataset) * self.data_cfg["train_split"])
153	            n_val = int(len(dataset) * self.data_cfg["val_split"])
154	            n_test = len(dataset) - n_train - n_val
155	            train_set, val_set, test_set = torch.utils.data.random_split(
156	                dataset, [n_train, n_val, n_test]
157	            )
158	
159	            self.train_loader = DataLoader(
160	                train_set,
161	                batch_size=self.data_cfg["batch_size"],
162	                shuffle=True,
163	                num_workers=0,
164	                collate_fn=collate_fn,
165	                pin_memory=False,
166	                drop_last=True,
167	            )
168	            self.val_loader = DataLoader(
169	                val_set,
170	                batch_size=self.data_cfg["batch_size"],
171	                shuffle=False,
172	                num_workers=0,
173	                collate_fn=collate_fn,
174	                pin_memory=False,
175	            )
176	
177	        # Update loss weights from stage config
178	        if "lambda_type" in stage_cfg:
179	            self.criterion.lambda_type = stage_cfg["lambda_type"]
180	            print(f"  lambda_type = {stage_cfg['lambda_type']}")
181	
182	        # Update Gumbel temperature if model supports it
183	        gumbel_temp = stage_cfg.get("gumbel_temperature", None)
184	        if gumbel_temp is not None and hasattr(self.model, 'dsp_cascade'):
185	            if hasattr(self.model.dsp_cascade, 'gumbel_temperature'):
186	                self.model.dsp_cascade.gumbel_temperature.fill_(gumbel_temp)
187	                print(f"  gumbel_temperature = {gumbel_temp}")
188	
189	        # Update learning rate scale for fine-tuning stages
190	        lr_scale = stage_cfg.get("learning_rate_scale", None)
191	        if lr_scale is not None:
192	            for pg in self.optimizer.param_groups:
193	                pg['lr'] = self.cfg["model"]["learning_rate"] * lr_scale
194	            print(f"  lr scaled to {self.optimizer.param_groups[0]['lr']:.2e}")
195	
196	        # Rebuild scheduler for this stage's epoch count
197	        stage_epochs = stage_cfg["epochs"]
198	        self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
199	            self.optimizer, T_max=stage_epochs, eta_min=1e-6
200	        )

202	    def _prepare_input(self, batch):
203	        """Get mel-spectrogram from precomputed cache or compute on-the-fly."""
204	        if "wet_mel" in batch:
205	            return batch["wet_mel"].to(self.device)
206	        wet_audio = batch["wet_audio"].to(self.device)
207	        mel_spec = self.frontend.mel_spectrogram(wet_audio)
208	        return mel_spec.squeeze(1)
209	
210	    def train_one_epoch(self, epoch):
211	        self.model.train()
212	        epoch_loss = 0.0
213	        n_batches = 0
214	
215	        for batch_idx, batch in enumerate(self.train_loader):
216	            mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
217	            target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device)
218	            target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device)
219	            target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device)
220	            target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device)
221	
222	            self.optimizer.zero_grad()
223	
224	            output = self.model(mel_frames)
225	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
226	
227	            target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
228	                target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
229	                n_fft=self.n_fft, filter_type=target_ft
230	            )
231	
232	            total_loss, components = self.criterion(
233	                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
234	                output["type_logits"], output["H_mag"],
235	                target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
236	                target_ft, target_H_mag,
237	            )
238	
239	            total_loss.backward()
240	            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(self.model.parameters(), max_norm=1.0)
241	            self.optimizer.step()
242	
243	            epoch_loss += total_loss.item()
244	            n_batches += 1
245	            self.global_step += 1
246	
247	            if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
248	                lr = self.optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]
249	                parts = [f"step={self.global_step} loss={total_loss.item():.4f} lr={lr:.2e}"]
250	                for k, v in components.items():
251	                    parts.append(f"{k}={v:.4f}")
252	                print(f"  [train] " + " | ".join(parts))
253	
254	        return epoch_loss / max(n_batches, 1)
255	
256	    @torch.no_grad()
257	    def validate(self, epoch):
258	        self.model.eval()
259	        val_loss = 0.0
260	        n_batches = 0
261	        param_maes = {"gain": [], "freq": [], "q": [], "type_acc": []}
262	
263	        for batch in self.val_loader:
264	            mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
265	            target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device)
266	            target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device)
267	            target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device)
268	            target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device)
269	
270	            output = self.model(mel_frames)
271	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
272	
273	            target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
274	                target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
275	                n_fft=self.n_fft, filter_type=target_ft
276	            )
277	
278	            total_loss, _ = self.criterion(
279	                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
280	                output["type_logits"], output["H_mag"],
281	                target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
282	                target_ft, target_H_mag,
283	            )
284	
285	            val_loss += total_loss.item()
286	            n_batches += 1
287	
288	            param_maes["gain"].append(
289	                (pred_gain - target_gain).abs().mean().item()
290	            )
291	            param_maes["freq"].append(
292	                (torch.log2(pred_freq / (target_freq + 1e-8))).abs().mean().item()
293	            )
294	            param_maes["q"].append(
295	                (torch.log10(pred_q / (target_q + 1e-8))).abs().mean().item()
296	            )
297	            param_maes["type_acc"].append(
298	                (output["filter_type"] == target_ft).float().mean().item()
299	            )
300	
301	        avg_val_loss = val_loss / max(n_batches, 1)
302	        metrics = {k: sum(v) / len(v) for k, v in param_maes.items() if v}
303	
304	        print(
305	            f"  [val] epoch={epoch} val_loss={avg_val_loss:.4f} "
306	            f"gain_mae={metrics.get('gain', 0):.2f}dB "
307	            f"freq_mae={metrics.get('freq', 0):.3f}oct "
308	            f"q_mae={metrics.get('q', 0):.3f}dec "
309	            f"type_acc={metrics.get('type_acc', 0):.1%}"
310	        )
311	        return avg_val_loss, metrics

313	    def save_checkpoint(self, epoch, val_loss, is_best=False, stage_name=""):
314	        ckpt_dir = Path("checkpoints")
315	        ckpt_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
316	        state = {

344	        global_epoch = 0

384	            for epoch in range(1, self.max_epochs + 1):
385	                t0 = time.time()
386	                train_loss = self.train_one_epoch(epoch)
387	                val_loss, metrics = self.validate(epoch)
388	                self.scheduler.step()
389	                elapsed = time.time() - t0
390	
391	                is_best = val_loss < self.best_val_loss
392	                if is_best:
393	                    self.best_val_loss = val_loss
394	                self.save_checkpoint(epoch, val_loss, is_best)
395	
396	                self.history.append({
397	                    "epoch": epoch,
398	                    "train_loss": train_loss,
399	                    "val_loss": val_loss,
400	                    "metrics": metrics,
401	                    "time_s": elapsed,
402	                })
403	
404	                print(
405	                    f"Epoch {epoch}/{self.max_epochs} ({elapsed:.1f}s) "
406	                    f"train={train_loss:.4f} val={val_loss:.4f}"
407	                )
408	
409	        hist_path = Path("checkpoints") / "training_history.json"
410	        with open(hist_path, "w") as f:

412	        print(f"\nTraining complete. Best val loss: {self.best_val_loss:.4f}")

1	"""
2	On-the-fly synthetic multi-type EQ dataset with optional precomputation.
3	
4	Generates (dry_audio, wet_audio, params) tuples with random multi-type
5	parametric EQ applied using the differentiable biquad cascade.
6	No external audio files needed — uses synthetic signals.
7	
8	Supports precompute mode to cache mel-spectrograms for faster training.
9	"""
10	import torch
11	import torch.utils.data as data
12	import numpy as np
13	import random
14	import math
15	from pathlib import Path
16	from differentiable_eq import (
17	    DifferentiableBiquadCascade,
18	    FILTER_PEAKING, FILTER_LOWSHELF, FILTER_HIGHSHELF,
19	    FILTER_HIGHPASS, FILTER_LOWPASS, NUM_FILTER_TYPES,
20	)
21	
22	FILTER_NAMES = ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
23	DEFAULT_TYPE_WEIGHTS = [0.5, 0.15, 0.15, 0.1, 0.1]
24	
25	
26	class SyntheticEQDataset(data.Dataset):
27	    """
28	    Generates synthetic (dry_audio, wet_audio, params) tuples on-the-fly.
29	    Applies random multi-type parametric EQ using the DifferentiableBiquadCascade.
30	
31	    Returns dict with wet_audio, dry_audio, gain, freq, q, filter_type tensors.
32	    """
33	
34	    def __init__(
35	        self,
36	        num_bands=5,
37	        sample_rate=44100,
38	        duration=3.0,
39	        n_fft=2048,
40	        size=50000,
41	        gain_range=(-24.0, 24.0),
42	        freq_range=(20.0, 20000.0),
43	        q_range=(0.1, 10.0),
44	        type_weights=None,
45	        signal_types=("noise", "sweep", "harmonic", "speech_like"),
46	        augment=True,
47	        precompute_mels=False,
48	        n_mels=128,
49	    ):
50	        self.num_bands = num_bands
51	        self.sample_rate = sample_rate
52	        self.num_samples = int(duration * sample_rate)
53	        self.n_fft = n_fft
54	        self.hop_length = n_fft // 4
55	        self._size = size
56	        self.gain_range = gain_range
57	        self.freq_range = freq_range
58	        self.q_range = q_range
59	        self.type_weights = type_weights or DEFAULT_TYPE_WEIGHTS
60	        self.signal_types = signal_types
61	        self.augment = augment
62	        self.n_mels = n_mels
63	        self.precompute_mels = precompute_mels
64	        self.dsp = DifferentiableBiquadCascade(
65	            num_bands=num_bands, sample_rate=sample_rate
66	        )
67	
68	        # Mel filterbank for precompute mode
69	        if precompute_mels:
70	            self._build_mel_filterbank()
71	
72	    def _build_mel_filterbank(self):
73	        """Build mel filterbank for precomputed mel-spectrograms."""
74	        n_freqs = self.n_fft // 2 + 1
75	        f_min = 20.0
76	        f_max = self.sample_rate / 2.0
77	
78	        def hz_to_mel(hz):
79	            return 2595.0 * np.log10(1.0 + hz / 700.0)
80	
81	        def mel_to_hz(mel):
82	            return 700.0 * (10.0 ** (mel / 2595.0) - 1.0)
83	
84	        f_min_mel = hz_to_mel(f_min)
85	        f_max_mel = hz_to_mel(f_max)
86	        mel_points = np.linspace(f_min_mel, f_max_mel, self.n_mels + 2)
87	        hz_points = mel_to_hz(mel_points)
88	        bin_points = ((self.n_fft + 1) * hz_points / self.sample_rate).astype(int)
89	
90	        fb = np.zeros((self.n_mels, n_freqs))
91	        for i in range(self.n_mels):
92	            f_left = bin_points[i]
93	            f_center = bin_points[i + 1]
94	            f_right = bin_points[i + 2]
95	            for j in range(f_left, f_center):
96	                if j < n_freqs and f_center > f_left:
97	                    fb[i, j] = (j - f_left) / (f_center - f_left)
98	            for j in range(f_center, f_right):
99	                if j < n_freqs and f_right > f_center:
100	                    fb[i, j] = (f_right - j) / (f_right - f_center)
101	
102	        self.mel_fb = torch.tensor(fb, dtype=torch.float32)
103	        self.mel_window = torch.hann_window(self.n_fft)
104	
105	    def __len__(self):
106	        return self._size
107	
108	    def _generate_dry_signal(self, signal_type):
109	        N = self.num_samples
110	        sr = self.sample_rate
111	        t = np.linspace(0, N / sr, N, endpoint=False)
112	
113	        if signal_type == "noise":
114	            audio = np.random.randn(N).astype(np.float32) * 0.3
115	        elif signal_type == "sweep":
116	            f0 = random.uniform(100, 2000)
117	            f1 = random.uniform(100, 2000)
118	            freq = np.linspace(f0, f1, N)
119	            phase = 2 * np.pi * np.cumsum(freq / sr)
120	            audio = (0.3 * np.sin(phase)).astype(np.float32)
121	        elif signal_type == "harmonic":
122	            audio = np.zeros(N, dtype=np.float32)
123	            n_harmonics = random.randint(3, 8)
124	            fundamental = random.uniform(80, 500)
125	            for h in range(1, n_harmonics + 1):
126	                amp = 0.2 / h
127	                audio += amp * np.sin(2 * np.pi * fundamental * h * t)
128	        elif signal_type == "speech_like":
129	            f0 = random.uniform(80, 300)
130	            audio = np.zeros(N, dtype=np.float32)
131	            for _ in range(random.randint(3, 12)):
132	                freq = random.uniform(f0 * 0.5, f0 * 4)
133	                amp = random.uniform(0.01, 0.1)
134	                dur = random.randint(int(0.01 * sr), int(0.15 * sr))
135	                onset = random.randint(0, max(1, N - dur))
136	                env = np.hanning(dur)
137	                audio[onset : onset + dur] += (
138	                    amp * env * np.sin(2 * np.pi * freq * np.arange(dur) / sr)
139	                )
140	        else:
141	            audio = np.random.randn(N).astype(np.float32) * 0.3
142	
143	        peak = np.max(np.abs(audio)) + 1e-8
144	        audio = audio / peak
145	        return audio
146	
147	    def _sample_multitype_params(self):
148	        """Sample multi-type EQ parameters with weighted filter type distribution."""
149	        gains = []
150	        freqs = []
151	        qs = []
152	        types = []
153	
154	        for _ in range(self.num_bands):
155	            ftype = random.choices(range(NUM_FILTER_TYPES), weights=self.type_weights, k=1)[0]
156	
157	            if ftype == FILTER_PEAKING:
158	                g = self._beta_gain()
159	                f = self._log_uniform(self.freq_range[0], self.freq_range[1])
160	                q = self._log_uniform(self.q_range[0], self.q_range[1])
161	            elif ftype == FILTER_LOWSHELF:
162	                g = self._beta_gain()
163	                f = self._log_uniform(max(20, self.freq_range[0]), min(5000, self.freq_range[1]))
164	                q = self._log_uniform(self.q_range[0], self.q_range[1])
165	            elif ftype == FILTER_HIGHSHELF:
166	                g = self._beta_gain()
167	                f = self._log_uniform(max(1000, self.freq_range[0]), min(20000, self.freq_range[1]))
168	                q = self._log_uniform(self.q_range[0], self.q_range[1])
169	            elif ftype == FILTER_HIGHPASS:
170	                g = 0.0
171	                f = self._log_uniform(20, 500)
172	                q = self._log_uniform(self.q_range[0], min(2.0, self.q_range[1]))
173	            elif ftype == FILTER_LOWPASS:
174	                g = 0.0
175	                f = self._log_uniform(2000, 20000)
176	                q = self._log_uniform(self.q_range[0], min(2.0, self.q_range[1]))
177	            else:
178	                g = random.uniform(*self.gain_range)
179	                f = self._log_uniform(self.freq_range[0], self.freq_range[1])
180	                q = self._log_uniform(self.q_range[0], self.q_range[1])
181	
182	            gains.append(g)
183	            freqs.append(f)
184	            qs.append(q)
185	            types.append(ftype)
186	
187	        # Sort by frequency for canonical ordering
188	        order = sorted(range(len(freqs)), key=lambda i: freqs[i])
189	        gains = [gains[i] for i in order]
190	        freqs = [freqs[i] for i in order]
191	        qs = [qs[i] for i in order]
192	        types = [types[i] for i in order]
193	
194	        return (
195	            torch.tensor(gains, dtype=torch.float32),
196	            torch.tensor(freqs, dtype=torch.float32),
197	            torch.tensor(qs, dtype=torch.float32),
198	            torch.tensor(types, dtype=torch.long),
199	        )
200	
201	    def _beta_gain(self):
202	        """Sample gain using beta distribution (concentrated near 0)."""
203	        sign = random.choice([-1, 1])
204	        magnitude = np.random.beta(2, 5) * min(abs(self.gain_range[0]), abs(self.gain_range[1]))
205	        return sign * magnitude
206	
207	    def _log_uniform(self, low, high):
208	        """Sample from log-uniform distribution."""
209	        return math.exp(random.uniform(math.log(low), math.log(high)))
210	
211	    def _apply_eq_freq_domain(self, dry_audio, gain_db, freq, q, filter_type):
212	        """Apply multi-type EQ in frequency domain."""
213	        window = torch.hann_window(self.n_fft)
214	        dry_tensor = dry_audio.unsqueeze(0)
215	        stft = torch.stft(
216	            dry_tensor, self.n_fft, self.hop_length, self.n_fft,
217	            window=window, return_complex=True,
218	        )
219	        wet_mag = self.dsp.apply_to_spectrum(
220	            torch.abs(stft), gain_db.unsqueeze(0), freq.unsqueeze(0),
221	            q.unsqueeze(0), filter_type=filter_type.unsqueeze(0)
222	        )
223	        phase = torch.angle(stft)
224	        wet_stft = wet_mag * torch.exp(1j * phase)
225	        wet_audio = torch.istft(
226	            wet_stft, self.n_fft, self.hop_length, self.n_fft,
227	            window=window, length=dry_audio.shape[0],
228	        )
229	        return wet_audio.squeeze(0)
230	
231	    def _audio_to_mel(self, audio):
232	        """Convert audio tensor to log-mel spectrogram. Returns (n_mels, T)."""
233	        stft = torch.stft(
234	            audio.unsqueeze(0), self.n_fft, self.hop_length, self.n_fft,
235	            window=self.mel_window, return_complex=True
236	        )
237	        mag = torch.abs(stft).squeeze(0)  # (F, T)
238	        mel = self.mel_fb @ mag  # (n_mels, T)
239	        mel = torch.clamp(mel, min=1e-8)
240	        return torch.log(mel)
241	
242	    def _cache_path(self):
243	        """Return NPZ cache file path based on dataset config."""
244	        from hashlib import md5
245	        cfg_str = f"{self._size}_{self.num_bands}_{self.sample_rate}_{self.num_samples}_{self.gain_range}_{self.freq_range}_{self.q_range}_{self.precompute_mels}_{self.n_mels}"
246	        h = md5(cfg_str.encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
247	        return Path("data_cache") / f"dataset_{self._size}_{h}.npz"
248	
249	    def precompute(self):
250	        """
251	        Pre-generate all samples and cache mel-spectrograms + params in memory.
252	        Saves/loads from NPZ file for instant reuse.
253	        """
254	        cache_path = self._cache_path()
255	
256	        # Try loading from disk cache
257	        if cache_path.exists():
258	            print(f"Loading cached dataset from {cache_path}...")
259	            data = np.load(cache_path, allow_pickle=True)
260	            self._cache = data["cache"].tolist()
261	            print(f"Loaded {len(self._cache)} samples from cache.")
262	            return
263	
264	        print(f"Precomputing {self._size} samples...")
265	        self._cache = []
266	        for i in range(self._size):
267	            sig_type = random.choice(self.signal_types)
268	            dry_audio = torch.from_numpy(self._generate_dry_signal(sig_type))
269	            gain_db, freq, q, filter_type = self._sample_multitype_params()
270	            wet_audio = self._apply_eq_freq_domain(dry_audio, gain_db, freq, q, filter_type)
271	
272	            if self.augment:
273	                scale = random.uniform(0.5, 1.0)
274	                wet_audio = wet_audio * scale
275	
276	            sample = {
277	                "gain": gain_db,
278	                "freq": freq,
279	                "q": q,
280	                "filter_type": filter_type,
281	            }
282	
283	            if self.precompute_mels:
284	                sample["wet_mel"] = self._audio_to_mel(wet_audio)
285	            else:
286	                sample["wet_audio"] = wet_audio
287	
288	            self._cache.append(sample)
289	            if (i + 1) % 2000 == 0:
290	                print(f"  {i+1}/{self._size}")
291	
292	        print(f"Precomputation complete: {len(self._cache)} samples cached.")
293	
294	        # Save to disk for instant reuse
295	        cache_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
296	        np.savez_compressed(cache_path, cache=np.array(self._cache, dtype=object))
297	        print(f"Dataset saved to {cache_path} ({cache_path.stat().st_size / 1e9:.2f} GB)")
298	
299	    def _generate_sample(self):
300	        """Generate a single training sample."""
301	        sig_type = random.choice(self.signal_types)
302	        dry_audio = torch.from_numpy(self._generate_dry_signal(sig_type))
303	
304	        gain_db, freq, q, filter_type = self._sample_multitype_params()
305	        wet_audio = self._apply_eq_freq_domain(dry_audio, gain_db, freq, q, filter_type)
306	
307	        if self.augment:
308	            scale = random.uniform(0.5, 1.0)
309	            wet_audio = wet_audio * scale
310	            dry_audio = dry_audio * scale
311	
312	        result = {
313	            "wet_audio": wet_audio,
314	            "dry_audio": dry_audio,
315	            "gain": gain_db,
316	            "freq": freq,
317	            "q": q,
318	            "filter_type": filter_type,
319	        }
320	
321	        if self.precompute_mels:
322	            result["wet_mel"] = self._audio_to_mel(wet_audio)
323	
324	        return result
325	
326	    def __getitem__(self, idx):
327	        if hasattr(self, '_cache') and idx < len(self._cache):
328	            return self._cache[idx]
329	        return self._generate_sample()
330	
331	
332	def collate_fn(batch):
333	    """Custom collate to pad variable-length audio and optional mel-spectrograms."""
334	    has_audio = "wet_audio" in batch[0]
335	    has_mel = "wet_mel" in batch[0]
336	
337	    def pad_audio(audio, target_len):
338	        if audio.shape[0] < target_len:
339	            audio = torch.nn.functional.pad(audio, (0, target_len - audio.shape[0]))
340	        return audio
341	
342	    def pad_mel(mel, target_len):
343	        if mel.shape[-1] < target_len:
344	            mel = torch.nn.functional.pad(mel, (0, target_len - mel.shape[-1]))
345	        return mel
346	
347	    gains = torch.stack([item["gain"] for item in batch])
348	    freqs = torch.stack([item["freq"] for item in batch])
349	    qs = torch.stack([item["q"] for item in batch])
350	    types = torch.stack([item["filter_type"] for item in batch])
351	
352	    result = {
353	        "gain": gains,
354	        "freq": freqs,
355	        "q": qs,
356	        "filter_type": types,
357	    }
358	
359	    if has_audio:
360	        wet_list = [item["wet_audio"] for item in batch]
361	        dry_list = [item["dry_audio"] for item in batch]
362	        max_len = max(x.shape[0] for x in wet_list)
363	        result["wet_audio"] = torch.stack([pad_audio(w, max_len) for w in wet_list])
364	        result["dry_audio"] = torch.stack([pad_audio(d, max_len) for d in dry_list])
365	        result["lengths"] = torch.tensor([w.shape[0] for w in wet_list])
366	
367	    if has_mel:
368	        mel_list = [item["wet_mel"] for item in batch]
369	        max_mel_len = max(m.shape[-1] for m in mel_list)
370	        result["wet_mel"] = torch.stack([pad_mel(m, max_mel_len) for m in mel_list])
371	
372	    return result
373

1	"""
2	Dataset for loading pre-generated multi-type EQ data.
3	Returns (wet_audio, dry_audio, params_tensor) where params_tensor
4	includes gain, freq, q, and filter type index per band.
5	"""
6	import os
7	import json
8	import torch
9	import torchaudio
10	from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
11	from pathlib import Path
12	
13	FILTER_TYPES = ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
14	TYPE_TO_INDEX = {t: i for i, t in enumerate(FILTER_TYPES)}
15	
16	
17	class SyntheticEQDataset(Dataset):
18	    """
19	    Dataset to load synthetic wet/dry audio and multi-type target parameters
20	    generated by generate_data.py.
21	    """
22	
23	    def __init__(self, data_dir, sample_rate=44100, duration=3.0):
24	        self.data_dir = Path(data_dir)
25	        self.sample_rate = sample_rate
26	        self.samples = int(sample_rate * duration)
27	
28	        # Discover all target param files
29	        self.files = list(self.data_dir.glob("*_params.json"))
30	
31	    def __len__(self):
32	        return len(self.files)
33	
34	    def __getitem__(self, idx):
35	        param_path = self.files[idx]
36	        file_stem = str(param_path).replace("_params.json", "")
37	
38	        wet_path = f"{file_stem}_wet.wav"
39	        dry_path = f"{file_stem}_dry.wav"
40	
41	        # Load audio
42	        wet_waveform, sr = torchaudio.load(wet_path)
43	        if sr != self.sample_rate:
44	            wet_waveform = torchaudio.functional.resample(wet_waveform, sr, self.sample_rate)
45	
46	        # Try loading dry audio (may not exist for older datasets)
47	        try:
48	            dry_waveform, _ = torchaudio.load(dry_path)
49	            if dry_waveform.shape[0] > 1:
50	                dry_waveform = torch.mean(dry_waveform, dim=0, keepdim=True)
51	        except Exception:
52	            dry_waveform = wet_waveform  # Fallback
53	
54	        # Ensure single channel and exact length
55	        if wet_waveform.size(0) > 1:
56	            wet_waveform = torch.mean(wet_waveform, dim=0, keepdim=True)
57	
58	        if wet_waveform.size(1) > self.samples:
59	            wet_waveform = wet_waveform[:, :self.samples]
60	        elif wet_waveform.size(1) < self.samples:
61	            wet_waveform = torch.nn.functional.pad(wet_waveform, (0, self.samples - wet_waveform.size(1)))
62	
63	        if dry_waveform.size(1) > self.samples:
64	            dry_waveform = dry_waveform[:, :self.samples]
65	        elif dry_waveform.size(1) < self.samples:
66	            dry_waveform = torch.nn.functional.pad(dry_waveform, (0, self.samples - dry_waveform.size(1)))
67	
68	        # Load parameters
69	        with open(param_path, 'r') as f:
70	            params = json.load(f)
71	
72	        # Convert to tensor: [gain, freq, q, type_idx] per band
73	        target_list = []
74	        for band in params:
75	            ftype_idx = TYPE_TO_INDEX.get(band.get("type", "peaking"), 0)
76	            target_list.append([
77	                band["gain"],
78	                band["freq"],
79	                band["q"],
80	                float(ftype_idx)
81	            ])
82	
83	        target_tensor = torch.tensor(target_list, dtype=torch.float32)
84	
85	        # Separate components
86	        gain = target_tensor[:, 0]
87	        freq = target_tensor[:, 1]
88	        q = target_tensor[:, 2]
89	        filter_type = target_tensor[:, 3].long()
90	
91	        return {
92	            "wet_audio": wet_waveform.squeeze(0),
93	            "dry_audio": dry_waveform.squeeze(0),
94	            "gain": gain,
95	            "freq": freq,
96	            "q": q,
97	            "filter_type": filter_type,
98	        }
99	
100	
101	def collate_fn(batch):
102	    """Custom collate to handle variable-length audio with padding."""
103	    wet_list = [item["wet_audio"] for item in batch]
104	    dry_list = [item["dry_audio"] for item in batch]
105	
106	    max_len = max(x.shape[0] for x in wet_list)
107	
108	    def pad_audio(audio, target_len):
109	        if audio.shape[0] < target_len:
110	            audio = torch.nn.functional.pad(audio, (0, target_len - audio.shape[0]))
111	        return audio
112	
113	    wet_padded = torch.stack([pad_audio(w, max_len) for w in wet_list])
114	    dry_padded = torch.stack([pad_audio(d, max_len) for d in dry_list])
115	    gains = torch.stack([item["gain"] for item in batch])
116	    freqs = torch.stack([item["freq"] for item in batch])
117	    qs = torch.stack([item["q"] for item in batch])
118	    types = torch.stack([item["filter_type"] for item in batch])
119	
120	    return {
121	        "wet_audio": wet_padded,
122	        "dry_audio": dry_padded,
123	        "gain": gains,
124	        "freq": freqs,
125	        "q": qs,
126	        "filter_type": types,
127	    }
128	
129	
130	def create_dataloaders(data_dir, batch_size=32, num_workers=4, split=(0.8, 0.1, 0.1)):
131	    """Create train/val/test dataloaders."""
132	    dataset = SyntheticEQDataset(data_dir)
133	
134	    train_len = int(len(dataset) * split[0])
135	    val_len = int(len(dataset) * split[1])
136	    test_len = len(dataset) - train_len - val_len
137	
138	    train_set, val_set, test_set = torch.utils.data.random_split(
139	        dataset, [train_len, val_len, test_len],
140	        generator=torch.utils.data.Generator().manual_seed(42)
141	    )
142	
143	    train_loader = DataLoader(train_set, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True,
144	                               num_workers=num_workers, collate_fn=collate_fn)
145	    val_loader = DataLoader(val_set, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False,
146	                             num_workers=num_workers, collate_fn=collate_fn)
147	    test_loader = DataLoader(test_set, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False,
148	                              num_workers=num_workers, collate_fn=collate_fn)
149	
150	    return train_loader, val_loader, test_loader

1	"""
2	Multi-type synthetic EQ data generation.
3	
4	Generates (dry, wet, params) pairs with support for peaking, low-shelf,
5	high-shelf, high-pass, and low-pass filter types.
6	"""
7	import os
8	import json
9	import random
10	import argparse
11	import math
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
14	
15	import numpy as np
16	import torch
17	import torchaudio
18	import torchaudio.functional as F
19	
20	
21	FILTER_TYPES = ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
22	DEFAULT_TYPE_WEIGHTS = [0.5, 0.15, 0.15, 0.1, 0.1]
23	
24	
25	def log_uniform(low, high):
26	    """Sample from log-uniform distribution (equal probability per decade)."""
27	    return math.exp(random.uniform(math.log(low), math.log(high)))
28	
29	
30	def beta_gain(max_gain=24.0):
31	    """
32	    Sample gain using beta distribution.
33	    Most EQ adjustments are small, so concentrate mass near 0.
34	    """
35	    sign = random.choice([-1, 1])
36	    magnitude = np.random.beta(2, 5) * max_gain
37	    return sign * magnitude
38	
39	
40	def sample_band_params(ftype, gain_bounds, freq_bounds, q_bounds):
41	    """Sample parameters for a single band based on filter type."""
42	    params = {"type": ftype}
43	
44	    if ftype == "peaking":
45	        params["gain"] = beta_gain(min(abs(gain_bounds[0]), abs(gain_bounds[1])))
46	        params["freq"] = log_uniform(freq_bounds[0], freq_bounds[1])
47	        params["q"] = log_uniform(q_bounds[0], q_bounds[1])
48	
49	    elif ftype == "lowshelf":
50	        params["gain"] = beta_gain(min(abs(gain_bounds[0]), abs(gain_bounds[1])))
51	        params["freq"] = log_uniform(max(20, freq_bounds[0]),
52	                                      min(5000, freq_bounds[1]))
53	        params["q"] = log_uniform(q_bounds[0], q_bounds[1])
54	
55	    elif ftype == "highshelf":
56	        params["gain"] = beta_gain(min(abs(gain_bounds[0]), abs(gain_bounds[1])))
57	        params["freq"] = log_uniform(max(1000, freq_bounds[0]),
58	                                      min(20000, freq_bounds[1]))
59	        params["q"] = log_uniform(q_bounds[0], q_bounds[1])
60	
61	    elif ftype == "highpass":
62	        params["gain"] = 0.0  # Not applicable
63	        params["freq"] = log_uniform(20, 500)
64	        params["q"] = log_uniform(q_bounds[0], min(2.0, q_bounds[1]))
65	
66	    elif ftype == "lowpass":
67	        params["gain"] = 0.0  # Not applicable
68	        params["freq"] = log_uniform(2000, 20000)
69	        params["q"] = log_uniform(q_bounds[0], min(2.0, q_bounds[1]))
70	
71	    return params
72	
73	
74	def generate_eq_params(num_bands, bounds, type_weights=None):
75	    """Generate random multi-type EQ parameters."""
76	    if type_weights is None:
77	        type_weights = DEFAULT_TYPE_WEIGHTS
78	
79	    params = []
80	    for _ in range(num_bands):
81	        ftype = random.choices(FILTER_TYPES, weights=type_weights, k=1)[0]
82	        band = sample_band_params(
83	            ftype,
84	            bounds["gain"],
85	            bounds["freq"],
86	            bounds["q"]
87	        )
88	        params.append(band)
89	
90	    # Sort by frequency for canonical ordering
91	    params.sort(key=lambda b: b["freq"])
92	
93	    return params
94	
95	
96	def compute_biquad_coeffs_peaking(gain_db, freq, q, sr):
97	    """Compute peaking biquad coefficients (Audio EQ Cookbook)."""
98	    A = 10.0 ** (gain_db / 40.0)
99	    w0 = 2.0 * math.pi * freq / sr
100	    alpha = math.sin(w0) / (2.0 * q)
101	
102	    b0 = 1.0 + alpha * A
103	    b1 = -2.0 * math.cos(w0)
104	    b2 = 1.0 - alpha * A
105	    a0 = 1.0 + alpha / A
106	    a1 = -2.0 * math.cos(w0)
107	    a2 = 1.0 - alpha / A
108	
109	    return [b0/a0, b1/a0, b2/a0, a1/a0, a2/a0]
110	
111	
112	def compute_biquad_coeffs_lowshelf(gain_db, freq, q, sr):
113	    """Compute low-shelf biquad coefficients."""
114	    A = 10.0 ** (gain_db / 40.0)
115	    w0 = 2.0 * math.pi * freq / sr
116	    alpha = math.sin(w0) / (2.0 * q)
117	    sqA = math.sqrt(A)
118	
119	    b0 = A * ((A + 1) - (A - 1) * math.cos(w0) + 2 * sqA * alpha)
120	    b1 = 2 * A * ((A - 1) - (A + 1) * math.cos(w0))
121	    b2 = A * ((A + 1) - (A - 1) * math.cos(w0) - 2 * sqA * alpha)
122	    a0 = (A + 1) + (A - 1) * math.cos(w0) + 2 * sqA * alpha
123	    a1 = -2 * ((A - 1) + (A + 1) * math.cos(w0))
124	    a2 = (A + 1) + (A - 1) * math.cos(w0) - 2 * sqA * alpha
125	
126	    return [b0/a0, b1/a0, b2/a0, a1/a0, a2/a0]
127	
128	
129	def compute_biquad_coeffs_highshelf(gain_db, freq, q, sr):
130	    """Compute high-shelf biquad coefficients."""
131	    A = 10.0 ** (gain_db / 40.0)
132	    w0 = 2.0 * math.pi * freq / sr
133	    alpha = math.sin(w0) / (2.0 * q)
134	    sqA = math.sqrt(A)
135	
136	    b0 = A * ((A + 1) + (A - 1) * math.cos(w0) + 2 * sqA * alpha)
137	    b1 = -2 * A * ((A - 1) + (A + 1) * math.cos(w0))
138	    b2 = A * ((A + 1) + (A - 1) * math.cos(w0) - 2 * sqA * alpha)
139	    a0 = (A + 1) - (A - 1) * math.cos(w0) + 2 * sqA * alpha
140	    a1 = 2 * ((A - 1) - (A + 1) * math.cos(w0))
141	    a2 = (A + 1) - (A - 1) * math.cos(w0) - 2 * sqA * alpha
142	
143	    return [b0/a0, b1/a0, b2/a0, a1/a0, a2/a0]
144	
145	
146	def compute_biquad_coeffs_highpass(freq, q, sr):
147	    """Compute high-pass biquad coefficients."""
148	    w0 = 2.0 * math.pi * freq / sr
149	    alpha = math.sin(w0) / (2.0 * q)
150	
151	    b0 = (1 + math.cos(w0)) / 2
152	    b1 = -(1 + math.cos(w0))
153	    b2 = (1 + math.cos(w0)) / 2
154	    a0 = 1 + alpha
155	    a1 = -2 * math.cos(w0)
156	    a2 = 1 - alpha
157	
158	    return [b0/a0, b1/a0, b2/a0, a1/a0, a2/a0]
159	
160	
161	def compute_biquad_coeffs_lowpass(freq, q, sr):
162	    """Compute low-pass biquad coefficients."""
163	    w0 = 2.0 * math.pi * freq / sr
164	    alpha = math.sin(w0) / (2.0 * q)
165	
166	    b0 = (1 - math.cos(w0)) / 2
167	    b1 = 1 - math.cos(w0)
168	    b2 = (1 - math.cos(w0)) / 2
169	    a0 = 1 + alpha
170	    a1 = -2 * math.cos(w0)
171	    a2 = 1 - alpha
172	
173	    return [b0/a0, b1/a0, b2/a0, a1/a0, a2/a0]
174	
175	
176	COEFF_FUNCS = {
177	    "peaking": lambda g, f, q, sr: compute_biquad_coeffs_peaking(g, f, q, sr),
178	    "lowshelf": lambda g, f, q, sr: compute_biquad_coeffs_lowshelf(g, f, q, sr),
179	    "highshelf": lambda g, f, q, sr: compute_biquad_coeffs_highshelf(g, f, q, sr),
180	    "highpass": lambda g, f, q, sr: compute_biquad_coeffs_highpass(f, q, sr),
181	    "lowpass": lambda g, f, q, sr: compute_biquad_coeffs_lowpass(f, q, sr),
182	}
183	
184	TYPE_TO_INDEX = {t: i for i, t in enumerate(FILTER_TYPES)}
185	
186	
187	def apply_eq_band(waveform, biquad_coeffs):
188	    """Apply a single biquad filter to a waveform using torchaudio lfilter."""
189	    b = torch.tensor(biquad_coeffs[:3], dtype=torch.float32).unsqueeze(0)
190	    a = torch.tensor([1.0] + biquad_coeffs[3:], dtype=torch.float32).unsqueeze(0)
191	    return F.lfilter(waveform, a, b, clamp=False)
192	
193	
194	def apply_eq_cascade(waveform, sample_rate, params):
195	    """Apply multi-type EQ cascade to dry audio."""
196	    wet = waveform.clone()
197	    for band in params:
198	        ftype = band["type"]
199	        gain = band.get("gain", 0.0)
200	        freq = band["freq"]
201	        q = band["q"]
202	
203	        coeffs = COEFF_FUNCS[ftype](gain, freq, q, sample_rate)
204	        wet = apply_eq_band(wet, coeffs)
205	
206	    return wet
207	
208	
209	def process_file(args):
210	    """Load dry audio, generate multi-type EQ params, apply, save."""
211	    file_path, out_dir, length_samples, sample_rate, bounds, num_bands, type_weights = args
212	    try:
213	        waveform, sr = torchaudio.load(file_path)
214	        if sr != sample_rate:
215	            waveform = F.resample(waveform, sr, sample_rate)
216	
217	        # Ensure single channel
218	        if waveform.size(0) > 1:
219	            waveform = torch.mean(waveform, dim=0, keepdim=True)
220	
221	        # Random crop or pad
222	        if waveform.size(1) > length_samples:
223	            start = random.randint(0, waveform.size(1) - length_samples)
224	            waveform = waveform[:, start:start + length_samples]
225	        elif waveform.size(1) < length_samples:
226	            pad_amount = length_samples - waveform.size(1)
227	            waveform = torch.nn.functional.pad(waveform, (0, pad_amount))
228	
229	        params = generate_eq_params(num_bands, bounds, type_weights)
230	        wet_audio = apply_eq_cascade(waveform, sample_rate, params)
231	
232	        # Save
233	        file_stem = Path(file_path).stem
234	        wet_path = os.path.join(out_dir, f"{file_stem}_wet.wav")
235	        dry_path = os.path.join(out_dir, f"{file_stem}_dry.wav")
236	        param_path = os.path.join(out_dir, f"{file_stem}_params.json")
237	
238	        torchaudio.save(wet_path, wet_audio, sample_rate)
239	        torchaudio.save(dry_path, waveform, sample_rate)
240	        with open(param_path, 'w') as f:
241	            json.dump(params, f, indent=2)
242	
243	        return True
244	    except Exception as e:
245	        print(f"Error processing {file_path}: {e}")
246	        return False
247	
248	
249	def build_dataset(input_dir, output_dir, config):
250	    """Iterate over dry audio files and generate multi-type EQ data."""
251	    input_paths = list(Path(input_dir).rglob("*.wav")) + list(Path(input_dir).rglob("*.flac"))
252	    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
253	
254	    length_samples = int(config['audio_duration'] * config['sample_rate'])
255	    bounds = {
256	        'gain': config.get('gain_bounds', [-24.0, 24.0]),
257	        'freq': config.get('freq_bounds', [20.0, 20000.0]),
258	        'q': config.get('q_bounds', [0.1, 10.0]),
259	    }
260	    type_weights = config.get('type_weights', DEFAULT_TYPE_WEIGHTS)
261	    num_bands = config.get('num_bands', 5)
262	    sample_rate = config.get('sample_rate', 44100)
263	
264	    tasks = [
265	        (str(p), output_dir, length_samples, sample_rate, bounds, num_bands, type_weights)
266	        for p in input_paths
267	    ]
268	
269	    print(f"Generating multi-type EQ data for {len(tasks)} files...")
270	    with Pool(processes=cpu_count()) as pool:
271	        results = pool.map(process_file, tasks)
272	
273	    success = sum(results)
274	    print(f"Successfully generated {success}/{len(tasks)} samples.")
275	
276	
277	if __name__ == "__main__":
278	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
279	    parser.add_argument("--input_dir", type=str, required=True, help="Path to dry audio files")
280	    parser.add_argument("--output_dir", type=str, required=True, help="Path to save wet audio and targets")
281	    args = parser.parse_args()
282	
283	    config = {
284	        "num_bands": 5,
285	        "sample_rate": 44100,
286	        "audio_duration": 3.0,
287	        "gain_bounds": [-24.0, 24.0],
288	        "freq_bounds": [20.0, 20000.0],
289	        "q_bounds": [0.1, 10.0],
290	        "type_weights": [0.5, 0.15, 0.15, 0.1, 0.1],
291	    }
292	    build_dataset(args.input_dir, args.output_dir, config)

1	"""
2	Streaming-compatible TCN encoder for blind EQ parameter estimation.
3	
4	Uses causal 1D convolutions with dilated temporal convolutional network (TCN)
5	architecture for real-time audio processing. Based on WaveNet-style gated
6	activations with residual and skip connections.
7	"""
8	import torch
9	import torch.nn as nn
10	import torch.nn.functional as F
11	from differentiable_eq import DifferentiableBiquadCascade, MultiTypeEQParameterHead
12	
13	
14	class CausalConv1d(nn.Module):
15	    """Causal 1D convolution with left-only padding (no look-ahead)."""
16	
17	    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, dilation=1):
18	        super().__init__()
19	        self.padding = (kernel_size - 1) * dilation
20	        self.conv = nn.Conv1d(
21	            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size,
22	            dilation=dilation, padding=0  # We handle padding manually
23	        )
24	
25	    def forward(self, x):
26	        # Left-only padding for causality
27	        x = F.pad(x, (self.padding, 0))
28	        return self.conv(x)
29	
30	
31	class GatedResidualBlock(nn.Module):
32	    """
33	    Gated activation block with residual and skip connections.
34	    Uses tanh * sigmoid gating (WaveNet-style).
35	    """
36	
37	    def __init__(self, channels, kernel_size=3, dilation=1):
38	        super().__init__()
39	        self.filter_conv = CausalConv1d(channels, channels, kernel_size, dilation)
40	        self.gate_conv = CausalConv1d(channels, channels, kernel_size, dilation)
41	
42	    def forward(self, x):
43	        z_filter = torch.tanh(self.filter_conv(x))
44	        z_gate = torch.sigmoid(self.gate_conv(x))
45	        return z_filter * z_gate
46	
47	
48	class TCNBlock(nn.Module):
49	    """
50	    Single TCN block: gated residual + 1x1 conv for skip/residual.
51	    """
52	
53	    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size=3, dilation=1):
54	        super().__init__()
55	        self.gated_block = GatedResidualBlock(in_channels, kernel_size, dilation)
56	
57	        # 1x1 convolutions for residual and skip connections
58	        self.residual_conv = nn.Conv1d(in_channels, out_channels, 1) \
59	            if in_channels != out_channels else nn.Identity()
60	        self.skip_conv = nn.Conv1d(in_channels, out_channels, 1)
61	
62	        self.bn = nn.BatchNorm1d(out_channels)
63	
64	    def forward(self, x):
65	        gated = self.gated_block(x)
66	        skip = self.skip_conv(gated)
67	        residual = self.residual_conv(x) + gated
68	        residual = self.bn(residual)
69	        return residual, skip
70	
71	
72	class TCNStack(nn.Module):
73	    """
74	    Stack of TCN blocks with exponentially increasing dilation.
75	    """
76	
77	    def __init__(self, channels, num_blocks, kernel_size=3, base_dilation=1):
78	        super().__init__()
79	        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList()
80	        for i in range(num_blocks):
81	            dilation = base_dilation * (2 ** i)
82	            self.blocks.append(TCNBlock(channels, channels, kernel_size, dilation))
83	
84	    def forward(self, x):
85	        skip_sum = 0
86	        for block in self.blocks:
87	            x, skip = block(x)
88	            skip_sum = skip_sum + skip
89	        return x, skip_sum
90	
91	
92	class CausalTCNEncoder(nn.Module):
93	    """
94	    Causal TCN encoder that processes mel-spectrogram frames sequentially.
95	
96	    Architecture: input projection → 2 TCN stacks → cumulative mean of
97	    skip connections → fixed-size embedding.
98	
99	    Supports both batch mode (full sequence) and streaming mode (frame-by-frame).
100	    """
101	
102	    def __init__(self, n_mels=128, embedding_dim=128, channels=128,
103	                 num_blocks=4, num_stacks=2, kernel_size=3):
104	        super().__init__()
105	        self.n_mels = n_mels
106	        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
107	        self.channels = channels
108	        self.num_blocks = num_blocks
109	        self.num_stacks = num_stacks
110	
111	        # Input projection: mel bins → channels
112	        self.input_proj = nn.Conv1d(n_mels, channels, 1)
113	
114	        # TCN stacks
115	        self.stacks = nn.ModuleList()
116	        for s in range(num_stacks):
117	            self.stacks.append(TCNStack(channels, num_blocks, kernel_size))
118	
119	        # Output projection
120	        self.output_proj = nn.Linear(channels, embedding_dim)
121	
122	    @property
123	    def receptive_field_frames(self):
124	        """Number of input frames needed for full receptive field."""
125	        # Each block has kernel_size=3, dilation doubles
126	        # Receptive field per block = (kernel_size - 1) * dilation
127	        # Total = sum over all blocks and stacks
128	        rf = 1
129	        for _ in range(self.num_stacks):
130	            for i in range(self.num_blocks):
131	                rf += (3 - 1) * (2 ** i)
132	        return rf
133	
134	    def forward(self, mel_frames):
135	        """
136	        Batch mode: process a sequence of mel frames.
137	
138	        Args:
139	            mel_frames: (Batch, n_mels, T) — sequence of mel-spectrogram frames
140	
141	        Returns:
142	            embedding: (Batch, embedding_dim) — aggregated embedding
143	            skip_sum: (Batch, channels, T) — per-frame skip connections
144	        """
145	        x = self.input_proj(mel_frames)
146	
147	        skip_total = 0
148	        for stack in self.stacks:
149	            x, skip = stack(x)
150	            skip_total = skip_total + skip
151	
152	        # Cumulative mean of skip connections over time
153	        # This gives a stable embedding that improves as more context arrives
154	        cumsum = skip_total.cumsum(dim=-1)
155	        counts = torch.arange(1, skip_total.shape[-1] + 1, device=skip_total.device).float()
156	        cummean = cumsum / counts.unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0)
157	
158	        # Use the last frame's cumulative mean as the embedding
159	        last_frame = cummean[:, :, -1]  # (Batch, channels)
160	        embedding = self.output_proj(last_frame)  # (Batch, embedding_dim)
161	
162	        return embedding, skip_total
163	
164	
165	class StreamingTCNModel(nn.Module):
166	    """
167	    Complete streaming model for blind EQ parameter estimation.
168	
169	    Combines a causal TCN encoder with multi-type EQ parameter head and
170	    differentiable biquad cascade. Supports both batch training and
171	    streaming inference modes.
172	    """
173	
174	    def __init__(self, n_mels=128, embedding_dim=128, num_bands=5,
175	                 channels=128, num_blocks=4, num_stacks=2,
176	                 sample_rate=44100, n_fft=2048):
177	        super().__init__()
178	        self.n_mels = n_mels
179	        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
180	        self.num_bands = num_bands
181	        self.sample_rate = sample_rate
182	        self.n_fft = n_fft
183	
184	        # Encoder
185	        self.encoder = CausalTCNEncoder(
186	            n_mels=n_mels,
187	            embedding_dim=embedding_dim,
188	            channels=channels,
189	            num_blocks=num_blocks,
190	            num_stacks=num_stacks,
191	        )
192	
193	        # Parameter head
194	        self.param_head = MultiTypeEQParameterHead(embedding_dim, num_bands)
195	
196	        # DSP layer
197	        self.dsp_cascade = DifferentiableBiquadCascade(num_bands, sample_rate)
198	
199	        # Streaming state
200	        self._streaming_buffer = None
201	        self._cumulative_skip_sum = None
202	        self._frame_count = 0
203	
204	    @property
205	    def receptive_field_frames(self):
206	        return self.encoder.receptive_field_frames
207	
208	    def reset_state(self):
209	        """Reset streaming state for a new inference session."""
210	        self._streaming_buffer = None
211	        self._cumulative_skip_sum = None
212	        self._frame_count = 0
213	
214	    def forward(self, mel_frames):
215	        """
216	        Batch-mode forward pass for training.
217	
218	        Args:
219	            mel_frames: (Batch, n_mels, T) — full mel-spectrogram sequence
220	
221	        Returns:
222	            dict with:

224	                type_logits: (Batch, num_bands, 5)
225	                type_probs: (Batch, num_bands, 5)
226	                filter_type: (Batch, num_bands)
227	                H_mag: (Batch, n_fft//2+1) — predicted frequency response
228	                embedding: (Batch, embedding_dim)
229	        """
230	        # 1. Encode
231	        embedding, skip_sum = self.encoder(mel_frames)
232	
233	        # 2. Predict parameters

235	            embedding, hard_types=not self.training
236	        )
237	
238	        # 3. Compute frequency response

241	        else:

245	            "params": (gain_db, freq, q),
246	            "type_logits": type_logits,

253	    def init_streaming(self, batch_size=1):
254	        """Initialize streaming state."""
255	        rf = self.receptive_field_frames
256	        self._streaming_buffer = torch.zeros(batch_size, self.n_mels, rf)
257	        self._cumulative_skip_sum = None
258	        self._frame_count = 0
259	
260	    def process_frame(self, mel_frame):
261	        """
262	        Streaming inference: process a single mel frame.
263	
264	        Args:
265	            mel_frame: (Batch, n_mels) — single mel-spectrogram frame
266	
267	        Returns:
268	            Same dict as forward(), with parameters estimated from
269	            cumulative context up to this frame.
270	        """
271	        if self._streaming_buffer is None:
272	            self.init_streaming(batch_size=mel_frame.shape[0])
273	
274	        # Shift buffer left and insert new frame
275	        self._streaming_buffer = torch.cat([
276	            self._streaming_buffer[:, :, 1:],
277	            mel_frame.unsqueeze(-1)
278	        ], dim=-1)
279	
280	        self._frame_count += 1
281	
282	        # Run encoder on buffer
283	        x = self.encoder.input_proj(self._streaming_buffer)
284

286	        for stack in self.encoder.stacks:
287	            x, skip = stack(x)

289	
290	        # Cumulative skip sum (streaming version)
291	        if self._cumulative_skip_sum is None:
292	            self._cumulative_skip_sum = skip_total
293	        else:
294	            self._cumulative_skip_sum = self._cumulative_skip_sum + skip_total
295	
296	        # Use running mean
297	        cummean = self._cumulative_skip_sum / self._frame_count
298	        last_frame = cummean[:, :, -1]
299	        embedding = self.encoder.output_proj(last_frame)

301	        # Predict parameters
302	        gain_db, freq, q, type_logits, type_probs, filter_type = self.param_head(
303	            embedding, hard_types=True

306	        H_mag = self.dsp_cascade(gain_db, freq, q, self.n_fft, filter_type)

309	            "params": (gain_db, freq, q),
310	            "type_logits": type_logits,
311	            "type_probs": type_probs,
312	            "filter_type": filter_type,

2	import torch.nn as nn
3	import torch.nn.functional as F

6	class STFTFrontend(nn.Module):
7	    """
8	    Differentiable STFT/iSTFT frontend.
9	    Extracts mel-spectrograms for the CNN encoder and provides
10	    complex STFT for the differentiable EQ filtering path.
11	    """
12	
13	    def __init__(
14	        self,
15	        n_fft=2048,
16	        hop_length=512,
17	        win_length=2048,
18	        mel_bins=128,
19	        sample_rate=22050,
20	        f_min=20.0,
21	        f_max=None,
22	        causal=False,
23	    ):
24	        super().__init__()
25	        self.n_fft = n_fft
26	        self.hop_length = hop_length
27	        self.win_length = win_length
28	        self.mel_bins = mel_bins
29	        self.sample_rate = sample_rate
30	        self.f_min = f_min
31	        self.f_max = f_max if f_max is not None else sample_rate // 2
32	        self.causal = causal
33	        self.register_buffer("window", torch.hann_window(win_length))
34	        self.register_buffer("mel_fb", self._build_mel_filterbank())
35	
36	    def _build_mel_filterbank(self):
37	        n_freqs = self.n_fft // 2 + 1
38	        f_min_mel = self._hz_to_mel(torch.tensor(self.f_min))
39	        f_max_mel = self._hz_to_mel(torch.tensor(self.f_max))
40	        mel_points = torch.linspace(f_min_mel, f_max_mel, self.mel_bins + 2)
41	        hz_points = self._mel_to_hz(mel_points)
42	        bin_points = ((self.n_fft + 1) * hz_points / self.sample_rate).long()
43	        fb = torch.zeros(self.mel_bins, n_freqs)
44	        for i in range(self.mel_bins):
45	            f_left = bin_points[i]
46	            f_center = bin_points[i + 1]
47	            f_right = bin_points[i + 2]
48	            for j in range(f_left, f_center):
49	                if j < n_freqs and f_center > f_left:
50	                    fb[i, j] = (j - f_left) / (f_center - f_left)
51	            for j in range(f_center, f_right):
52	                if j < n_freqs and f_right > f_center:
53	                    fb[i, j] = (f_right - j) / (f_right - f_center)
54	        return fb
55	
56	    @staticmethod
57	    def _hz_to_mel(hz):
58	        return 2595.0 * torch.log10(1.0 + hz / 700.0)
59	
60	    @staticmethod
61	    def _mel_to_hz(mel):
62	        return 700.0 * (10.0 ** (mel / 2595.0) - 1.0)
63	
64	    def stft(self, audio):
65	        """
66	        audio: (Batch, Time)
67	        Returns complex STFT: (Batch, FreqBins, TimeFrames)
68	        """
69	        if self.causal:
70	            # Left-only padding for causal operation
71	            pad_amount = self.win_length - self.hop_length
72	            audio = F.pad(audio, (pad_amount, 0))
73	            return torch.stft(
74	                audio,
75	                self.n_fft,
76	                self.hop_length,
77	                self.win_length,
78	                window=self.window,
79	                return_complex=True,
80	                center=False,
81	            )
82	        return torch.stft(
83	            audio,
84	            self.n_fft,
85	            self.hop_length,
86	            self.win_length,
87	            window=self.window,
88	            return_complex=True,
89	            center=True,
90	        )
91	
92	    def istft(self, complex_stft, length=None):
93	        """
94	        complex_stft: (Batch, FreqBins, TimeFrames)
95	        Returns audio: (Batch, Time)
96	        """
97	        return torch.istft(
98	            complex_stft,
99	            self.n_fft,
100	            self.hop_length,
101	            self.win_length,
102	            window=self.window,
103	            length=length,
104	            center=True,
105	        )
106	
107	    def mel_spectrogram(self, audio):
108	        """
109	        audio: (Batch, Time)
110	        Returns log-mel spectrogram: (Batch, 1, MelBins, TimeFrames)
111	        """
112	        complex_spec = self.stft(audio)
113	        mag = torch.abs(complex_spec)
114	        mel_spec = torch.matmul(self.mel_fb, mag)
115	        mel_spec = torch.clamp(mel_spec, min=1e-8)
116	        log_mel = torch.log(mel_spec)
117	        return log_mel.unsqueeze(1)
118	
119	    def get_magnitude(self, audio):
120	        """
121	        audio: (Batch, Time)
122	        Returns magnitude STFT: (Batch, FreqBins, TimeFrames)
123	        """
124	        return torch.abs(self.stft(audio))
125	
126	    def get_complex(self, audio):
127	        """
128	        audio: (Batch, Time)
129	        Returns complex STFT: (Batch, FreqBins, TimeFrames)
130	        """
131	        return self.stft(audio)
132	
133	    def forward(self, audio):
134	        """
135	        Full frontend: returns both mel-spectrogram for CNN
136	        and complex STFT for differentiable filtering.
137	        audio: (Batch, Time)
138	        Returns:
139	            mel_spec: (Batch, 1, MelBins, TimeFrames)
140	            complex_stft: (Batch, FreqBins, TimeFrames)
141	            audio_length: int
142	        """
143	        mel_spec = self.mel_spectrogram(audio)
144	        complex_stft = self.stft(audio)
145	        return mel_spec, complex_stft, audio.shape[-1]
146	
147	
148	def apply_eq_to_complex_stft(complex_stft, H_mag):
149	    """
150	    Apply an EQ magnitude response to a complex STFT.
151	    Only modifies magnitudes; preserves phase.
152	    complex_stft: (Batch, FreqBins, TimeFrames) complex
153	    H_mag: (Batch, FreqBins) or (Batch, FreqBins, 1)
154	    Returns: modified complex STFT (same shape)
155	    """
156	    original_mag = torch.abs(complex_stft)
157	    phase = torch.angle(complex_stft)
158	    if H_mag.dim() == 2:
159	        H_mag = H_mag.unsqueeze(-1)
160	    new_mag = original_mag * H_mag
161	    return new_mag * torch.exp(1j * phase)
162
